steps.prompt: Which words are the finite verbs?

hints.Venient only: 'Recumbent' is also a finite verb meaning 'they will recline'
hints.recumbent only: 'Venient' is also a finite verb meaning 'they will come'
hints.oriente and occidente: 'Oriente' and 'occidente' are present participles used as nouns (east/west)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'veniunt' — 'Venient' has the future -ie- formation
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'veniant' — 'Venient' is future indicative

hints.vendo, vendere, vendidi, venditum: These are the parts of 'vendere' (to sell)
hints.venor, venari, venatus sum, —: These are the parts of 'venari' (to hunt)
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the parts of 'videre' (to see)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'oriens' — 'oriente' with -e is ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'orientem' — 'oriente' is ablative after 'ab'

steps.target: present participle of 'orior' (to rise), used as a noun meaning 'east'
choices: present participle of 'orior' (to rise), used as a noun meaning 'east'
a 3rd declension noun unrelated to any verb

hints.a 3rd declension noun unrelated to any v: 'Oriens' is the present participle of 'orior' (to rise) — the sun rises in the east
hints.a gerundive meaning 'to be risen': A gerundive would be 'oriendus' — 'oriens/oriente' is a present participle
hints.an adjective meaning 'oriental': 'Oriens' is a substantivized participle, not primarily an adjective

hints.to lie down (to sleep): 'To lie down' is 'cubare' — 'recumbere' specifically means 'to recline at a banquet'
hints.to count again: 'To count again' is 'renumerare' — 'recumbere' means 'to recline at table'

steps.reveal: recumbent = they will recline (at the banquet table)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'recumbunt' — 'recumbent' has the future -e- vowel (3rd conj.)
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'recumbant' — 'recumbent' is future indicative

hints.dative: 'Regno' could be dative, but after 'in' it is ablative of place

translation: They will come from east and west and will recline in the kingdom of God
